_comment: Enhanced tool descriptions with parameter examples per Vertex AI best practices
_bestPractices: Function descriptions should clearly describe what the function does
Parameter descriptions should be detailed and include examples
Use thought signatures with function calling for best results
Model decides when to call functions based on descriptions

playMusic.description: PLAYS AUDIO DIRECTLY in the current voice session. Use when user says: 'play music', 'play some music', 'play a song', 'put on music', 'I want to hear music', 'can you play [song/artist/genre]'. This tool starts audio playback immediately - it does NOT contact anyone, does NOT reach out to anyone, does NOT message anyone. Just plays music directly through the speaker. Searches music catalog and starts playback. Returns track information.

parameters.query: Music search query. Can be: song title ('Bohemian Rhapsody'), artist name ('The Beatles'), genre ('jazz'), mood ('relaxing', 'chill', 'upbeat'), or combination ('calm late night music'). DO NOT put contact names here - this is for music search terms only.

reachOut.description: Contact a PERSON via call, text, or email. Use ONLY when user wants to communicate with a human contact like 'text Mom', 'call John', 'message my friend'. This is for HUMAN communication only. Do NOT use for music - use playMusic instead. Do NOT use for playing audio.

parameters.contact: Name of a PERSON to contact. Must be a real human contact like 'Mom', 'John Smith', 'my boss'. NOT for music or audio playback.
parameters.purpose: Why contacting this person: 'wish happy birthday', 'check in', 'ask a question'. NOT for playing music or audio.

rememberAboutUser.description: Store an important fact about the user with semantic embedding for future recall. Facts persist across all sessions and are searchable by meaning. Stores personal details, life events, goals, concerns, and preferences.

parameters.fact: The fact to remember with full context. Examples: 'has two kids named Maya and Jake, ages 8 and 12', 'retiring next year from teaching biology', 'worried about healthcare costs', 'loves hiking in Colorado mountains'

recallFromMemory.description: Semantic search across stored memories. Finds memories by meaning, not just keywords. Returns matches with temporal context showing when the memory was stored.

parameters.topic: What to recall - conceptual, not exact match. Examples: 'retirement plans', 'family situation', 'their concerns', 'career goals'

getWeather.description: Get current weather conditions or forecast. Call when user asks about weather, temperature, or outdoor conditions. Returns temperature, conditions, and recommendations.

parameters.location: Location for weather. Can be city name, zip code, or 'current' for user's location. Examples: 'San Francisco', '94102', 'current', 'New York City'
parameters.type: Type of weather info. Options: 'current' (right now), 'forecast' (upcoming days), 'hourly' (next 24 hours)

handoffToMaya.description: Transfer conversation to Maya Santos, who specializes in habits, routines, productivity, and behavior change.

parameters.reason: Brief context for the handoff. Examples: 'wants to build a morning routine', 'struggling with exercise', 'breaking phone addiction'

handoffToAlex.description: Transfer conversation to Alex Chen, who specializes in communication, email writing, calendar management, and professional correspondence.

parameters.reason: Brief context for the handoff. Examples: 'drafting an important email', 'salary negotiation prep', 'improving communication'

handoffToPeter.description: Transfer conversation to Peter John, who specializes in research, data analysis, stock market insights, and financial trends.

parameters.reason: Brief context for the handoff. Examples: 'researching a stock', 'understanding market trends', 'investment analysis'

handoffToJordan.description: Transfer conversation to Jordan Taylor, who specializes in life planning, event organization, celebrations, and milestones.

parameters.reason: Brief context for the handoff. Examples: 'planning a birthday', 'organizing a reunion', 'wedding planning'

handoffToNayan.description: Transfer conversation to Nayan Patel (Premium), who specializes in wisdom, philosophical perspective, meaning-making, and spiritual guidance.

parameters.reason: Brief context for the handoff. Examples: 'seeking life perspective', 'discussing purpose', 'existential questioning'

createAppointment.description: Create a new calendar appointment or meeting. Call when user wants to schedule something - meetings, reminders, events. Requires confirmation before creating.

parameters.title: Name of the appointment. Examples: 'Doctor appointment', 'Team standup', 'Call with Mom', 'Dentist checkup'
parameters.date: Date of appointment. Natural language or ISO format. Examples: 'tomorrow at 2pm', 'next Monday', '2025-01-15T14:00:00'
parameters.duration: How long the appointment lasts. Examples: '30 minutes', '1 hour', '90 minutes'
parameters.location: Optional: Where the appointment takes place. Examples: 'Zoom', 'Conference room B', 'Dr. Smith's office'

sendMessage.description: Send a text message or email to someone. HIGH STAKES - requires user confirmation before sending. Call when user explicitly asks to send a message to someone.

parameters.recipient: Who to send to. Can be name or contact. Examples: 'Mom', 'John Smith', 'my boss'
parameters.message: The content of the message. Should be composed based on user's intent.

getCurrentTime.description: Get the current time in a specific timezone or the user's local timezone. Call when user asks what time it is.

parameters.timezone: Optional: Timezone to get time for. Examples: 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Tokyo', 'local' for user's timezone

addHabit.description: Create a new habit for the user to track. Call when user wants to start building a new habit or tracking a behavior.

parameters.name: Name of the habit. Short and clear. Examples: 'Morning meditation', 'Drink 8 glasses of water', 'Read for 30 minutes', 'Exercise'
parameters.frequency: How often. Examples: 'daily', 'weekdays', 'Mon/Wed/Fri', '3 times per week'

getCrisisResources.description: Get crisis support resources and hotlines. Call IMMEDIATELY when user expresses suicidal thoughts, self-harm, or is in crisis. Safety first - this tool provides professional resources.

parameters.location: Optional: Location for local resources. Examples: 'California', 'UK', 'Canada'

groundingExercise.description: Guide user through a grounding exercise to help with anxiety, panic, or overwhelming emotions. Call when user seems distressed and needs to calm down.

parameters.type: Type of exercise. Options: '5-4-3-2-1' (senses), 'breathing' (deep breaths), 'body-scan' (physical awareness), 'quick' (60 seconds)

searchNews.description: Search for recent news on a topic. Call when user wants to know what's happening in the world or get updates on a specific subject.

parameters.query: News topic to search. Examples: 'stock market today', 'AI developments', 'local weather alerts', 'sports scores'

getMarketSummary.description: Get current stock market summary including major indices. Call when user asks about how the market is doing.

parameters.detail: Level of detail. Options: 'brief' (just indices), 'full' (with movers), 'sector' (by sector)

setTimer.description: Set a countdown timer. Call when user wants to be reminded after a certain duration.

parameters.duration: How long to wait. Natural language. Examples: '5 minutes', '1 hour', '30 seconds', '2 hours and 15 minutes'
parameters.label: Optional: What the timer is for. Examples: 'pasta cooking', 'break time', 'meeting starts'

calculateTip.description: Calculate tip amount and total for a bill. Call when user wants help figuring out a tip.

parameters.split: Optional: Number of people splitting. Examples: 2, 4

wrapUpConversation.description: Gracefully wrap up the conversation. Call when user indicates they need to go or the conversation is naturally concluding.

paradoxOfTheDay.description: Get today's philosophical paradox for contemplation and reflection. Call when user wants philosophical depth, asks about life meaning, seeks wisdom, or seems ready for deeper thinking. Returns a thought-provoking paradox with gentle guidance.

parameters.action: What to do. Options: 'get-paradox' (receive today's), 'reflect' (share thoughts on it), 'request-new' (get a different one)

questionBeneath.description: Guide user through the '5 Whys' exploration to discover the deeper question beneath their surface concern. Call when user has a question or concern that might have deeper roots, or when helping them understand their true motivations.

parameters.initialQuestion: The user's starting question or concern. Examples: 'Should I change jobs?', 'Why am I always tired?', 'Is this relationship right for me?'

inboxZeroChallenge.description: Start an inbox management challenge with streak tracking. Call when user wants help managing their email, reducing inbox clutter, or building better email habits.

parameters.action: What to do. Options: 'start' (begin challenge), 'check-in' (daily progress), 'status' (current streak)

sundayPrepGame.description: Guide user through a weekly planning ritual for the week ahead. Call when user wants to prepare for the week, do weekly planning, or set intentions for upcoming days.

parameters.action: What to do. Options: 'start' (begin planning), 'review' (check last week), 'complete' (finish session)

lifePortfolioReview.description: Conduct a quarterly review of major life domains (career, relationships, health, finance, personal growth). Call when user wants to reflect on life balance, do a life audit, or assess how different areas are going.

predictionMarket.description: Help user predict outcomes in their own life and track accuracy over time. Call when user is making decisions, wants to calibrate their intuition, or enjoys thinking probabilistically about the future.

parameters.action: What to do. Options: 'predict' (make new prediction), 'review' (check past predictions), 'resolve' (record outcome)

compoundInterestGame.description: Play Maya's habit tracking game that shows compound growth from small daily actions. Call when user wants motivation for habit building, to see how small actions add up, or needs encouragement for consistency.

parameters.action: What to do. Options: 'start' (begin game), 'track' (record today's action), 'status' (see compound progress)

logMood.description: Record user's current emotional state for tracking over time. Call when user shares how they're feeling and it would be valuable to track the pattern.

parameters.intensity: How intense the feeling is. Scale 1-10.
parameters.note: Optional: Context or what triggered the mood. Examples: 'good meeting at work', 'didn't sleep well', 'nice walk outside'

addGoal.description: Create a new goal for the user to track progress toward. Call when user expresses an aspiration, wants to achieve something, or sets an intention.

parameters.title: Name of the goal. Clear and motivating. Examples: 'Run a 5K', 'Save $10,000', 'Learn Spanish basics', 'Read 24 books this year'
parameters.targetDate: Optional: When to achieve by. Examples: 'December 2025', 'in 6 months', 'end of year'
parameters.targetValue: Optional: Numeric target. Examples: 10000 (for saving), 26.2 (for marathon miles)

addTask.description: Create a task or to-do item. Call when user mentions something they need to do, a reminder they want, or an action item.

parameters.title: What needs to be done. Action-oriented. Examples: 'Call dentist for appointment', 'Buy birthday gift for Mom', 'Review quarterly report'
parameters.dueDate: When it's due. Natural language. Examples: 'today', 'tomorrow', 'next Friday', 'in 3 days'

trackHabit.description: Record that user completed a habit for today. Call when user mentions they did something that's part of their tracked habits.

parameters.habitName: Which habit was done. Partial match works. Examples: 'meditation', 'exercise', 'reading', 'water'

identifyBoundaryNeeds.description: Help user identify areas where they need to set boundaries. Call when user mentions feeling drained, overwhelmed by others, inability to say no, or feeling like a pushover. Uses Four Tendencies framework for personalized guidance.

parameters.situation: The situation or relationship causing strain. Examples: 'my mother calls every day', 'boss expects overtime constantly', 'friend always asks for favors'

setBoundary.description: Help user script and practice setting a specific boundary. Provides exact words and role-play practice. Call when user needs help with the actual conversation or language.

parameters.situation: Specific situation. Examples: 'they keep borrowing money', 'won't respect my work hours', 'criticizes my choices'

practiceBoundaryScript.description: Practice saying boundary-setting words out loud. Role-play the conversation. Call when user wants to rehearse before having the actual conversation.

parameters.personType: Who they're setting boundary with. Options: 'boss', 'family', 'friend', 'partner', 'coworker'

handleBoundaryPushback.description: Help user navigate pushback when someone resists their boundary. Call when user reports the other person got upset, guilt-tripped, or didn't accept the boundary.

buildConversationSkills.description: Help user improve their conversation and social skills. Call when user feels awkward socially, doesn't know what to say, struggles at parties, or wants to make friends.

parameters.challenge: What specifically is hard. Examples: 'starting conversations', 'keeping them going', 'feeling natural'

practiceSmallTalk.description: Practice casual conversation with role-play scenarios. Call when user wants to rehearse social interactions before an event.

navigateSocialAnxiety.description: Help user cope with social anxiety before or during social situations. Call when user expresses nervousness about upcoming social events.

parameters.situation: What's coming up. Examples: 'work party tomorrow', 'meeting new people', 'presentation'

developFriendships.description: Help user build deeper friendships or make new friends. Call when user mentions loneliness or wanting closer connections.

understandAnger.description: Help user understand their anger patterns. Call when user is angry, mentions losing temper, or wants to manage anger better.

identifyAngerTriggers.description: Help user identify what triggers their anger. Call when user wants to understand why they get angry or prevent future outbursts.

parameters.recentExample: Recent anger episode. Examples: 'blew up at my partner yesterday', 'got furious at a driver'

angerCoolingTechniques.description: Provide immediate anger management techniques. Call when user is currently angry and needs to calm down NOW.

repairAfterAnger.description: Help user repair relationship damage after an anger outburst. Call when user regrets how they acted while angry.

parameters.situation: What happened. Examples: 'yelled at my kids', 'said hurtful things to partner', 'sent angry email to boss'

understandProcrastination.description: Help user understand why they procrastinate. Call when user can't start tasks, keeps putting things off, or feels stuck.

parameters.task: What they're avoiding. Examples: 'big project at work', 'cleaning the house', 'making a phone call'

breakDownTask.description: Break overwhelming task into tiny actionable steps. Call when user is procrastinating because task feels too big.

parameters.task: The overwhelming task. Examples: 'file taxes', 'write thesis', 'organize garage'

buildMomentum.description: Help user build momentum through small wins. Call when user is stuck and needs to get started with anything.

parameters.context: What they're trying to do. Examples: 'working on a project', 'getting back into exercise', 'job searching'

overcomeProcrastinationBlock.description: Help user push through a specific procrastination block right now. Call when user wants to start but can't.

recognizePerfectionism.description: Help user recognize perfectionist patterns. Call when nothing is ever good enough, they can't finish things, or they're paralyzed by high standards.

parameters.pattern: What they're experiencing. Examples: 'rewriting emails 10 times', 'can't ship anything', 'harsh self-criticism'

challengePerfectionistThoughts.description: Challenge specific perfectionist thoughts. Call when user expresses all-or-nothing thinking about their work or self.

parameters.thought: The perfectionist thought. Examples: 'if it's not perfect no one will respect me', 'I should be able to do this perfectly'

embraceGoodEnough.description: Practice accepting good enough over perfect. Call when user needs permission to release something that's not perfect.

parameters.situation: What needs to be good-enough. Examples: 'this presentation', 'my house before guests', 'my parenting'

assessScreenTime.description: Assess user's digital habits and screen time concerns. Call when user mentions phone addiction, doomscrolling, or too much screen time.

parameters.concern: Main concern. Examples: 'on phone all day', 'can't stop scrolling', 'affects my sleep', 'losing time to social media'

createDigitalBoundaries.description: Help user set boundaries with technology. Call when user wants to limit app usage or create tech-free times.

parameters.app: What to limit. Examples: 'social media', 'TikTok', 'work email', 'phone overall'

addressDoomscrolling.description: Help user break doomscrolling habit. Call when user mentions compulsive scrolling, especially of negative news.

assessBurnout.description: Assess user's burnout level and symptoms. Call when user mentions exhaustion, can't keep up, depleted, or thinks they're burning out.

parameters.symptoms: What they're experiencing. Examples: 'exhausted all the time', 'cynical about work', 'nothing feels meaningful'

createRecoveryPlan.description: Help create a burnout recovery plan. Call when user is in burnout and needs a path forward.

parameters.priorities: What matters most. Examples: 'keeping my job', 'my health', 'my family', 'not sure'

identifyEnergyDrains.description: Help identify what's draining the user's energy. Call when user feels depleted and wants to understand why.

exploreBodyImage.description: Help user explore their relationship with their body. Call when user expresses body dissatisfaction, negative self-talk about appearance, or body image struggles.

parameters.feeling: What they're experiencing. Examples: 'hate how I look', 'uncomfortable in my body', 'compare myself to others'

buildBodyGratitude.description: Help build appreciation for what the body does (vs. how it looks). Call when user needs to shift from appearance to function/capability.

clarifyDatingGoals.description: Help user get clear on what they want in dating/relationships. Call when user is confused about what they want, just starting to date, or unsure about relationship goals.

buildDatingConfidence.description: Help build user's dating confidence. Call when user feels unworthy of love, nervous about dating, or lacks confidence.

parameters.concern: What's holding them back. Examples: 'not attractive enough', 'don't know how to flirt', 'fear of rejection'

navigateDatingAnxiety.description: Help manage dating anxiety before or during dates. Call when user is nervous about an upcoming date or dating situation.

parameters.situation: What's causing anxiety. Examples: 'first date tomorrow', 'waiting for text back', 'asking someone out'

processRejection.description: Help user process dating rejection. Call when user was rejected, ghosted, or turned down.

parameters.situation: What happened. Examples: 'got ghosted', 'they said they just want to be friends', 'date went badly'

understandNeurodivergence.description: Help user understand their neurodivergent experience. Call when user mentions ADHD, autism, dyslexia, or feeling like their brain works differently.

buildNeurodivergentStrategies.description: Build personalized strategies for neurodivergent challenges. Call when user needs coping strategies for their specific brain.

parameters.challenge: What they need help with. Options: 'focus', 'organization', 'time', 'social', 'overwhelm', 'routines'

celebrateNeurodivergentStrengths.description: Celebrate and leverage neurodivergent strengths. Call when user needs to see the positive side of how their brain works.

assessTraumaReadiness.description: Assess user's readiness to explore trauma-related topics. SAFETY CRITICAL. Call BEFORE any deeper trauma work to ensure they have support and grounding.

parameters.safety: Current safety status. Examples: 'doing okay', 'having a hard time', 'feel stable today'

buildSafetyResources.description: Help user build trauma-safe resources and coping strategies. Call when user needs grounding tools or safety planning.

navigateTraumaTriggers.description: Help user navigate when they've been triggered. Call when user is activated/triggered and needs to regulate.

parameters.trigger: What triggered them. Examples: 'saw something on TV', 'argument reminded me', 'anniversary of event'

exploreIntimacyNeeds.description: Help user explore their intimacy needs and desires. Call when user wants to talk about closeness, vulnerability, or connection in relationships.

navigateIntimacyFears.description: Help user navigate fears around intimacy. Call when user is scared of getting close, being vulnerable, or being truly seen.

parameters.fear: What they're afraid of. Examples: 'being rejected', 'being hurt again', 'losing myself', 'being too much'

communicateIntimacyNeeds.description: Help user communicate intimacy needs to partner. Call when user needs to talk to partner about closeness, touch, or emotional needs.

parameters.need: What they need to communicate. Examples: 'need more emotional connection', 'want different physical intimacy', 'need space'

manageChronicCondition.description: Support user in managing life with a chronic condition. Call when user mentions chronic illness, chronic pain, or ongoing health condition.

processChronicConditionEmotions.description: Help process emotions around chronic illness. Call when user expresses grief, frustration, or difficulty accepting their condition.

parameters.emotion: What they're feeling. Examples: 'angry at my body', 'grieving my old life', 'frustrated with limitations'

navigateFlareUps.description: Help user cope during a condition flare-up. Call when user reports their condition is worse or they're having a bad health day.

parameters.situation: Current state. Examples: 'pain is really bad today', 'exhausted and frustrated', 'symptoms acting up'

exploreMidlifeQuestions.description: Help user explore midlife questions and transitions. Call when user questions their life choices, wonders 'is this all there is', or mentions midlife crisis.

parameters.question: What they're grappling with. Examples: 'what have I accomplished', 'is this the right path', 'what do I want the rest of my life to look like'

redefineSuccess.description: Help user redefine what success means for them now. Call when user's old definition of success no longer fits.

parameters.oldDefinition: What success used to mean. Examples: 'career achievement', 'making money', 'being the best'

buildMidlifeMeaning.description: Help user find meaning and purpose in midlife. Call when user wants to figure out what matters now or build a meaningful second half of life.

processBreakupPain.description: Help user process breakup pain and grief. Call when user just went through a breakup, divorce, or relationship ending.

navigateBreakupEmotions.description: Help navigate the emotional waves after breakup. Call when user is hit by waves of emotion related to breakup.

buildPostBreakupIdentity.description: Help user rebuild identity after relationship ends. Call when user doesn't know who they are without their ex.

moveForwardFromBreakup.description: Help user move forward and date again after breakup. Call when user is ready (or questioning if ready) to start dating again.

getWeatherHabitInsights.description: Analyze weather and environmental conditions to suggest habit adjustments. Returns proactive insights like 'Rainy day - try indoor workout instead.' Can fetch user's real habits if userId provided.

parameters.location: City name for weather analysis. Examples: 'Philadelphia', 'Denver', 'San Francisco'
parameters.userId: Optional: User ID to fetch their actual tracked habits. If provided, will check their fitness/outdoor habits against conditions.
parameters.userHabits: Optional: List of habit names if userId not provided. Examples: ['running', 'cycling', 'yoga']

getHabitRecommendation.description: Get a personalized recommendation for a specific habit based on current weather and environmental conditions. Use when user asks 'should I run today?' or 'is it good for outdoor yoga?'

parameters.location: City name for weather check. Examples: 'Philadelphia', 'New York'
parameters.habitName: Name of the habit to check. Examples: 'running', 'cycling', 'outdoor yoga', 'hiking'

analyzeNewsMoodImpact.description: Analyze news headlines for emotional impact and determine best delivery approach. Better than human - protects user from overwhelming news when stressed.

parameters.newsHeadlines: Array of news headlines to analyze for mood impact

getPositiveNewsOnly.description: Filter news to show only positive, uplifting stories. Use when user needs a mood boost or explicitly wants to avoid heavy news.

parameters.newsHeadlines: Array of news headlines to filter for positive content

shouldSkipNews.description: Determine if news should be skipped based on user mood and news content. Returns recommendation and reason.

getCommuteSuggestions.description: Get personalized suggestions for making commute time productive or enjoyable. Suggests podcasts, audiobooks, pep talks based on commute length and context.

parameters.commuteTime: Expected commute time in minutes. Examples: 15, 30, 45, 60
parameters.hasUpcomingMeeting: Whether user has a meeting shortly after arrival - triggers pep talk suggestions

suggestPreMeetingPepTalk.description: Offer a pep talk or confidence boost before an important meeting during commute. Use when user mentions nervousness about upcoming meeting.

parameters.meetingType: Type of meeting. Examples: 'presentation', 'interview', 'client call', 'performance review'

addRelationship.description: Add a new relationship/contact to track. Use when user mentions a friend, family member, or important person they want to remember. Stores birthdays, interests, favorite teams for future insights.

parameters.name: Person's name. Examples: 'John Smith', 'Mom', 'Sarah from work'
parameters.birthdayMonth: Birthday month (1-12). Optional but enables birthday reminders.
parameters.birthdayDay: Birthday day (1-31). Optional but enables birthday reminders.
parameters.interests: Their interests/hobbies for gift suggestions. Examples: ['reading', 'golf', 'cooking']
parameters.favoriteTeams: Their favorite sports teams for 'friend's team won' alerts. Examples: ['Eagles', 'Lakers', 'Yankees']

getRelationshipInfo.description: Get information about a specific relationship/contact including birthday, interests, favorite teams, and last contact date.

parameters.name: Name of the person to look up

recordContact.description: Record that user had contact with someone. Use when they mention they talked to, called, or met with someone. Helps track relationship health.

parameters.name: Name of the person they contacted

getUpcomingBirthdays.description: Get upcoming birthdays from tracked relationships. Better than human - we never forget a birthday! Use proactively or when user asks about birthdays.

getContactReminders.description: Get reminders about people user hasn't contacted in a while. Better than human - we track relationship health and gently nudge. Use for relationship check-ins.

getGiftSuggestions.description: Get gift suggestions for a person based on their tracked interests. Use when user needs gift ideas for birthdays, holidays, or special occasions.

getRelationshipInsights.description: Get all relationship insights - birthdays, contact reminders, team updates ('friend's team won!'). Use for comprehensive relationship health check-in.

addInterestToRelationship.description: Add an interest to a person's profile. Helps with gift suggestions and conversation topics.

parameters.name: Name of the person
parameters.interest: The interest to add. Examples: 'cooking', 'hiking', 'sci-fi movies', 'gardening'

addFavoriteTeamToRelationship.description: Add a favorite sports team to a person's profile. Enables 'Your friend's team won!' notifications.

parameters.name: Name of the person
parameters.team: The sports team to add. Examples: 'Eagles', 'Lakers', 'Yankees', 'Manchester United'

enhanced._comment_superhuman: Peter's Superhuman Research Tools - 'Better than Human' quantitative analysis

recordDecision.description: Record a decision with full context (options, factors, emotional state) for pattern analysis. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Tracks every decision for objective quality analysis over time.

parameters.context: What decision are you facing? Examples: 'Should I take the job offer?', 'Buy vs lease car'
parameters.options: Array of options you're considering. Examples: ['Accept offer', 'Decline', 'Negotiate']

recordDecisionOutcome.description: Record the outcome of a previous decision for learning analysis.

parameters.decisionId: ID of the decision to update

analyzeDecisionQuality.description: Analyze your decision patterns - what times/conditions lead to best outcomes.

recordSleepData.description: Track sleep for correlation with decisions, energy, and performance. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Finds hidden patterns in YOUR data.

analyzeSleepCorrelations.description: Analyze how YOUR sleep affects decisions, energy, and mood. Uses YOUR historical data.

recordEnergyLevel.description: Track energy throughout the day for peak performance analysis.

parameters.factors: What might have contributed (e.g., ['good sleep', 'light meal'])

predictEnergy.description: Predict your energy at a specific time based on YOUR patterns.

analyzePeakPerformance.description: Identify YOUR peak performance times for creative, analytical, and decision-making work.

calculateLifestyleImpact.description: Calculate how a specific lifestyle factor impacts YOUR outcomes.

scoreEvidenceQuality.description: Evaluate evidence quality using scientific methodology. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Applies rigorous research standards.

synthesizeResearch.description: Synthesize multiple research sources into coherent findings. Like having a research assistant.

findCounterArguments.description: Find legitimate counter-arguments to a claim. BETTER THAN HUMAN: No confirmation bias.

parameters.claim: The claim to find counter-arguments for

verifyClaim.description: Verify a health, financial, or factual claim against evidence.

getBaseRate.description: Get base rate statistics for a scenario. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Provides objective probabilities.

parameters.scenario: The scenario to get base rates for. Examples: 'startup success', 'marriage lasting'

recordGoalProgress.description: Track progress toward a goal for prediction modeling.

predictGoalSuccess.description: Predict probability of achieving a goal based on YOUR historical patterns.

parameters.goalId: ID of the goal to analyze

projectBehavioralTrajectory.description: Project where a habit or behavior will lead over time.

recordHabit.description: Record habit completion for survival analysis.

analyzeHabitSurvival.description: Analyze which habits survive and why. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Statistical survival analysis on YOUR data.

analyzeCounterfactual.description: Analyze 'what if' scenarios based on your data. 'What if I had slept 8 hours instead of 6?'

predictLifeEventImpact.description: Predict how a life event will impact your patterns (job change, move, etc.).

parameters.domain: What domain to predict impact on. Examples: 'productivity', 'relationships', 'finances'

analyzeSECFiling.description: Analyze SEC filings with institutional-grade research. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Reads full filings, extracts key insights.

trackInsiderTrading.description: Track insider trading activity for a stock. See what executives are doing with their shares.

analyzeOptionsFlow.description: Analyze unusual options activity. See what institutional traders are betting on.

bridgeMacroToPersonal.description: Connect macro economic events to YOUR personal situation. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Personalized economic analysis.

designExperiment.description: Design a personal A/B test with proper methodology. Test what actually works for YOU.

parameters.hypothesis: What you want to test (e.g., 'Morning exercise improves my focus')
parameters.intervention: What you'll change (e.g., '30 min morning workout')

recordExperimentData.description: Record a data point for a running experiment.

analyzeExperiment.description: Analyze your experiment results with statistical rigor.

createBelief.description: Create a Bayesian belief to track and update with evidence.

parameters.statement: The belief statement (e.g., 'I am a morning person')

updateBelief.description: Update a belief with new evidence using Bayesian reasoning.

trackHypothesis.description: Track a hypothesis about yourself for systematic testing.

updateHypothesis.description: Update hypothesis status with evidence or conclusion.

detectConfounds.description: Detect confounding variables in observed correlations. BETTER THAN HUMAN: Objective bias detection.

parameters.observation: The correlation you observed (e.g., 'When I exercise, I sleep better')

calculateEffectSize.description: Calculate effect size for a before/after comparison. Know if a change actually matters.

parameters.beforeValues: Array of measurements before the change
parameters.afterValues: Array of measurements after the change

getLocalEconomics.description: Get local economic indicators for your area.

synthesizeIndustryTrends.description: Synthesize trends in a specific industry.

analyzeNewsSentiment.description: Analyze sentiment in news about a topic or company.

recordSpending.description: Record spending to calculate YOUR personal inflation rate.

calculatePersonalInflation.description: Calculate YOUR personal inflation rate vs official CPI. See how YOUR costs compare.

trackRelationship.description: Track a relationship for network analysis.

logInteraction.description: Log an interaction for relationship health analysis.

analyzeCommunicationPatterns.description: Analyze your communication patterns. Who do you reach out to when stressed? Who energizes you?

scoreRelationshipHealth.description: Score the health of a specific relationship.

mapInfluence.description: Map who influences you in different life domains.

analyzeNetworkGaps.description: Identify gaps in your support network. Where do you need more support?

holdParadox.description: Track contradictory desires or values without trying to resolve them. Nayan holds both truths indefinitely. Use when someone expresses wanting two things that seem to conflict. Remembers the paradox for future reference.

parameters.desire1: First desire or value they want. Example: 'stability', 'career success', 'independence'
parameters.desire2: Contradictory desire or value. Example: 'adventure', 'family time', 'connection'
parameters.context: What surfaced this paradox. Example: 'talking about job offer', 'discussing relationship'
parameters.howLongHeld: How long they've felt this tension. Example: 'years', 'since the kids were born', 'always'

mortalityPerspective.description: Provide concrete mortality awareness for clarity. Not morbid—clarifying. The Stoic memento mori made personal. Use when someone is caught up in short-term concerns or needs perspective on what matters.

parameters.currentConcern: What they are worried about or focused on. Example: 'work deadline', 'relationship drama', 'financial stress'
parameters.ageIfKnown: User's age if known for more accurate calculations

generatePersonalKoan.description: Generate a personalized paradox or koan designed to break specific thinking patterns. Questions that dissolve problems rather than solve them. Use when someone is stuck in overthinking or a mental loop.

parameters.stuckPattern: The thinking pattern or problem they are stuck in. Example: 'perfectionism', 'fear of failure', 'analysis paralysis'
parameters.whatTheyveTrieds: What approaches they've already tried. Example: 'making lists', 'talking to friends', 'research'

trackEnough.description: Record when someone declares something is 'enough' and surface it when goalposts move. Hold the mirror to endless striving. Use when someone states what would satisfy them, or to check past statements.

parameters.domain: What domain this 'enough' relates to. Options: 'money', 'career', 'achievement', 'possessions', 'status', 'relationship', 'other'
parameters.enoughStatement: What they said would be enough. Example: '$100k salary', 'manager title', 'a loving relationship'
parameters.isRecording: True to record new 'enough' statement, false to check past statements against current

ancestralWisdom.description: Help connect to the wisdom of ancestors and lineage. What did they know that we have forgotten? Use when someone faces a challenge their ancestors also faced, or needs to feel connected to something larger.

parameters.currentChallenge: What they are facing. Example: 'starting over', 'financial hardship', 'loss', 'major decision'
parameters.knownAncestorExperience: Any known experiences of grandparents/ancestors. Example: 'grandmother survived the war', 'grandfather immigrated alone'

trackWisdomIncubation.description: Track things the user needs to 'sit with' and return to them at the right moment. Perfect patience for things that need time. Use when someone explicitly says they need time with a question, or to check on incubating items.

parameters.question: The question or decision they need to sit with. Example: 'whether to leave my job', 'how I feel about having kids', 'what I want from life'
parameters.suggestedDuration: How long this might need. Options: 'days', 'weeks', 'months', 'until-ready'
parameters.checkIn: True if checking on incubating items, false if recording new one

trackHabitDNA.description: Build a complete genetic profile of a habit: what makes it live, what kills it, optimal conditions. Use whenever a habit event occurs (started, maintained, struggled, broke, restarted, mastered) or to retrieve habit history.

parameters.habitName: Name of the habit. Example: 'morning meditation', 'daily exercise', 'journaling'
parameters.context: Context around this event. Example: 'traveling', 'stressful week', 'after promotion'
parameters.triggerOrBarrier: What helped or hurt. Example: 'accountability partner', 'weekend', 'evening fatigue'
parameters.timeOfDay: When this happens. Example: 'morning', 'after lunch', 'before bed'
parameters.checkHistory: True to retrieve full habit history instead of recording

mapFriction.description: Track exactly where, when, and why habits encounter friction. Build a map of willpower failure points. Use when they mention struggling with a habit.

parameters.habitName: Which habit encountered friction. Example: 'gym', 'reading', 'cooking at home'
parameters.description: What specifically caused the friction. Example: 'too tired after work', 'gym is too far', 'no healthy food in house'
parameters.intensity: How much it derailed you. Options: 'minor', 'moderate', 'major'
parameters.viewMap: True to view friction map instead of recording new point

assessTendency.description: Dynamically assess their Four Tendency (Upholder/Questioner/Obliger/Rebel) from behavior patterns. Use when you observe tendency signals or they want to see their profile.

parameters.context: Context of the signal. Example: 'they mentioned needing a gym buddy', 'they broke their own deadline'
parameters.getProfile: True to get current tendency profile

detectKeystone.description: Identify keystone habits that cascade into other positive behaviors. Find their highest-leverage habits. Use when they mention one habit affecting others.

parameters.observation: Observation about habit correlations. Example: 'when I exercise, I eat better', 'good sleep makes me more productive'
parameters.primaryHabit: The habit that might be keystone. Example: 'exercise', 'making the bed', 'morning routine'
parameters.affectedHabits: Habits that improve when primary habit happens. Example: ['eating healthy', 'drinking water', 'mood']
parameters.viewKeystones: True to view identified keystone habits

trackIdentityShift.description: Track evolution of identity statements over time. 'I am someone who...' is the deepest form of habit change. Use when they express identity or to view their evolution.

parameters.statement: Identity statement. Example: 'I am someone who exercises', 'I am someone who saves money', 'I am a reader'
parameters.confidence: How true it feels. Options: 'aspiring' (want to be), 'emerging' (starting to feel true), 'established' (mostly true), 'core' (just who I am)
parameters.viewEvolution: True to view identity evolution over time

analyzeSetbackPattern.description: Deep pattern analysis of setbacks across years. Find common threads in every time they've fallen off. Use when they have a setback or want to see patterns.

parameters.habitName: Which habit had a setback. Example: 'gym', 'meditation', 'no sugar'
parameters.whatHappened: What caused the setback. Example: 'skipped one day and never went back', 'vacation threw me off'
parameters.emotionalTrigger: Emotional state before the setback. Example: 'stressed', 'bored', 'lonely', 'anxious'
parameters.viewPatterns: True to analyze patterns across all setbacks

conductHabitAutopsy.description: Deep post-mortem analysis for habits that died. Learn from every failure to prevent future deaths. Use when a habit has completely stopped.

parameters.habitName: The habit that died. Example: 'daily running', 'journaling', 'no screens before bed'
parameters.howLongItLasted: How long the habit survived. Example: '2 weeks', '3 months', 'almost a year'
parameters.causeOfDeath: What killed it. Example: 'travel', 'injury', 'lost motivation', 'too ambitious'
parameters.lastRites: When did you last do it. Example: 'last month', 'before the holidays', 'before I moved'
parameters.lessonsLearned: What you learned. Example: 'started too big', 'needed accountability', 'wrong time of day'
parameters.willResurrect: Do you want to try this habit again someday?

recallEventPatterns.description: Perfect memory for patterns across ALL events - budget tendencies, guest dynamics, emotional patterns, vendor preferences. Use to learn from past events or record new patterns.

parameters.recordPattern: New pattern to record: {eventName, pattern, lesson}

getGuestInsights.description: Permanent guest profiles - dietary needs, accessibility, attendance patterns, relationship dynamics. Never forget a guest's needs.

parameters.guestName: Name of guest to look up. Example: 'Aunt Martha', 'John Smith'
parameters.updateGuest: Update profile: {name, dietary, accessibility, note, avoidSeatingWith}
parameters.listAll: True to list all known guests

detectMilestones.description: Detect celebrations humans forget - work anniversaries, friendship milestones, quiet win streaks, approaching transitions.

captureEventMeaning.description: Remember what events MEANT, not just logistics - the emotional journey, meaningful speeches, lessons learned.

parameters.meaning: What this event meant. Example: 'First time dad was proud of me'
parameters.lessonLearned: What you learned from this event
parameters.recallEvent: True to recall meaning of past event

checkCelebrationHealth.description: Track celebration balance - are they celebrating enough? Too much? Always for others? Notice joy gaps.

parameters.getBalance: True to get celebration balance report

anticipateTransition.description: See life transitions coming from conversation patterns - empty nest, retirement, career change. Suggest planning before they ask.

checkPlanningReadiness.description: Cross-team readiness check before major planning - financial health, calendar capacity, energy levels, emotional readiness.

parameters.eventType: What are you planning? Example: 'wedding', 'vacation', 'big purchase'

revealBlindSpot.description: Surface patterns they may be avoiding or not seeing. Hold up a mirror to what they can't see themselves.

parameters.observation: What pattern you've noticed they're avoiding
parameters.recordNew: True to record a new blind spot observation
parameters.viewBlindSpots: True to view known blind spots

simulateCounterfactual.description: Explore 'what if' scenarios based on past decisions. Learn from alternate paths not taken.

parameters.originalDecision: The decision that was made. Example: 'took the safe job instead of startup'
parameters.alternativePath: What they could have done instead. Example: 'joined the startup'

predictPattern.description: Project where current patterns are heading. See the trajectory before it arrives.

parameters.pattern: The pattern being tracked. Example: 'spending on subscriptions'

scoreDecision.description: Rate decision quality over time. Learn what kinds of decisions you make well (and poorly).

parameters.decision: The decision made. Example: 'bought the expensive car'

findCorrelation.description: Discover correlations across different life domains that you couldn't see yourself.

detectAnomaly.description: Flag unusual patterns that break from normal behavior. Sometimes anomalies are warnings, sometimes opportunities.

parameters.anomaly: What unusual pattern was detected. Example: 'spending 3x normal on restaurants'

archiveInsight.description: Store important insights for future reference. Build a personal knowledge base of what you've learned about yourself.

parameters.insight: The insight to archive. Example: 'I make better decisions in the morning'

listRoutines.description: Show what Ferni does automatically for the user. Call when user asks: 'what do you do for me?', 'show my routines', 'what are you taking care of?', 'list my automations', 'what's set up?'. Returns all active and paused routines with their triggers.

createRoutine.description: Set up a new automated routine for the user. Call when user says: 'remind me every morning to...', 'when I get home do...', 'set up a routine', 'every day at 7am...', 'can you check in with me each night?'. Creates scheduled messages, phrase triggers, or location-based routines.

parameters.name: Friendly name for the routine. Examples: 'Morning check-in', 'Evening gratitude', 'Water reminder'
parameters.triggerType: What starts this routine. Options: 'time' (scheduled), 'phrase' (voice command), 'location' (arriving/leaving)
parameters.triggerValue: The trigger details. For time: '7:00 AM' or '19:30'. For phrase: 'good morning' or 'time for bed'. For location: 'home' or 'office'
parameters.action: What Ferni should say or do. Examples: 'Check in about how you slept', 'Remind you to drink water', 'Ask about your day'

runRoutine.description: Manually trigger a routine right now. Call when user says: 'run my morning routine', 'do my check-in now', 'trigger the water reminder'. Executes all actions in the routine immediately.

parameters.routineName: Name of the routine to run. Example: 'Morning check-in', 'Evening gratitude'

toggleRoutine.description: Pause or resume an automated routine. Call when user says: 'pause my morning routine', 'stop doing the water reminders', 'resume my check-ins', 'turn off the evening routine'. Keeps the routine but stops/starts it running.

parameters.routineName: Name of the routine. Example: 'Morning check-in'
parameters.action: Whether to pause or resume. Options: 'pause', 'resume'

removeRoutine.description: Permanently delete an automated routine. Call when user says: 'delete my morning routine', 'remove that routine', 'I don't want the water reminders anymore'. This is permanent - the routine is gone.

parameters.routineName: Name of the routine to delete. Example: 'Morning check-in'

suggestRoutines.description: Suggest routine ideas the user might like. Call when user asks: 'what could you do for me?', 'give me routine ideas', 'what routines do you recommend?', 'help me automate something'. Returns popular template suggestions.

surfaceRelevantMemory.description: Proactively surface relevant memories based on the current conversation context. BETTER THAN HUMAN: automatically recalls related information from past conversations without being asked. Returns memories that enrich the current discussion.

parameters.context: Current conversation context or topic to find relevant memories for. Examples: 'discussing career change', 'talking about family', 'planning vacation'

predictUserNeed.description: Predict what the user might need based on patterns, time of day, and conversation context. BETTER THAN HUMAN: anticipates needs before they're expressed. Returns predicted needs with confidence levels.

parameters.context: Current conversation context. Examples: 'morning check-in', 'discussing stress', 'end of workday'

musicControl.description: Control music playback - pause, resume, skip, adjust volume. Use when user says 'pause the music', 'skip this song', 'turn it up', 'next track', 'stop playing'. Direct control over currently playing music.

parameters.volume: Optional: Volume level 0-100. Use when user says 'turn it up/down' or specifies a level

musicInfo.description: Get information about currently playing music - track name, artist, album, progress. Use when user asks 'what's playing?', 'who sings this?', 'what song is this?'. Returns current track details.

playMusicInRoom.description: Play music in a specific room or speaker. Use when user says 'play music in the kitchen', 'play in bedroom', 'start music on living room speaker'. For multi-room audio systems like Sonos.

parameters.room: Name of the room or speaker. Examples: 'kitchen', 'living room', 'bedroom', 'office'
parameters.query: Optional: What to play. If not specified, resumes current music. Examples: 'jazz', 'my playlist', 'The Beatles'

transferToRoom.description: Transfer currently playing music to a different room or speaker. Use when user says 'move the music to the bedroom', 'transfer to kitchen', 'switch to the living room speaker'.

parameters.targetRoom: Room to transfer music to. Examples: 'kitchen', 'bedroom', 'patio'

setRoomVolume.description: Set volume for a specific room or speaker. Use when user says 'turn down the kitchen', 'make the bedroom quieter', 'set living room to 50%'.

parameters.room: Room or speaker name. Examples: 'kitchen', 'bedroom', 'living room'

listMusicRooms.description: List all available rooms/speakers in the multi-room audio system. Use when user asks 'what speakers do I have?', 'show my rooms', 'where can I play music?'. Returns available Sonos/smart speaker zones.

syncMusicRooms.description: Sync multiple rooms to play the same music together. Use when user says 'play everywhere', 'sync all rooms', 'party mode', 'group the speakers'. Creates a synchronized audio group.

parameters.rooms: Optional: Array of room names to sync. If not provided, syncs all available rooms. Examples: ['kitchen', 'living room']

discoverSpotifyDevices.description: Discover available Spotify Connect devices for playback. Use when user wants to know what devices they can play on, or troubleshooting playback issues. Returns list of active Spotify devices.

searchAppleMusic.description: Search Apple Music catalog for songs, artists, albums, or playlists. Use when user wants to find music on Apple Music specifically. Returns search results with playable content.

parameters.query: What to search for. Examples: 'Taylor Swift', 'chill vibes playlist', 'Abbey Road album', 'workout songs'

timeInCity.description: Get the current time in a specific city. Use when user asks 'what time is it in Tokyo?', 'time in London', 'what's the time in New York?'. Returns the time and day in that city.

parameters.city: City name to get the time for. Examples: 'Tokyo', 'London', 'New York', 'Sydney', 'Paris'

bestTimeToCall.description: Find a good time to call someone in another timezone. Use when user says 'when should I call London?', 'best time to reach Tokyo', 'what time works for someone in Sydney?'. Calculates overlapping reasonable hours.

parameters.theirCity: City where the other person is. Examples: 'Tokyo', 'London', 'Sydney'
parameters.yourTime: Optional: Your proposed time to check. Examples: '9am', '2:30pm', '18:00'

searchWeb.description: Search the web for information. EXECUTE SILENTLY - don't announce 'let me search', just call and share results naturally. Use when user asks factual questions, wants to know about something, or needs to look something up.

parameters.query: What to search for. Examples: 'capital of France', 'who invented the telephone', 'latest iPhone model'

searchWikipedia.description: Look up information on Wikipedia. Use for factual questions, learning about topics, or getting encyclopedic information.

parameters.query: Topic to look up. Examples: 'Quantum computing', 'Marie Curie', 'Renaissance art'

defineTerm.description: Define a word or concept. Use when user asks 'what does X mean?', 'define Y', 'what is Z?'. Returns a clear definition.

parameters.term: The word or concept to define. Examples: 'ephemeral', 'blockchain', 'cognitive dissonance'

searchRecipes.description: Search for recipes and cooking instructions. Use when user asks for recipe ideas, how to make a dish, or cooking instructions.

parameters.dish: The dish to find a recipe for. Examples: 'chicken parmesan', 'chocolate cake', 'vegan tacos'
